name: C/C++ Compiler (clang)
description: CONSTRUCT: compile C/C++/ObjC source into a native artifact (exe / .o / .s / LLVM IR) with clang (falls back to cc/gcc). For building PoCs, test harnesses, stubs, and shared libs rekit needs. Cross-compiles via --target/--arch. Compiles only — does NOT run the result (feed the output to exec-observe/emulate-code yourself). BYO C compiler.

check: command -v clang || command -v cc || command -v gcc
prerequisites.install_hint: Install a C compiler: clang (macOS: xcode-select --install; Linux: apt/dnf install clang or LLVM) or gcc.

safety.notes: CONSTRUCT skill — produces a binary, never runs it. executes_input:no (the sample isn't executed). Caveat: compiling UNTRUSTED source still invokes a compiler (preprocessor/pragmas), a mild risk distinct from running a sample — hence tier 1 not 0. The typical input is your own PoC source (trusted). To run what it builds, hand the artifact to the dynamic tier explicitly.

args.name: --out
args.desc: output path (default derived from source + emit)

payload.note: BYO clang (or cc/gcc). Seed of the CONSTRUCT kind — rekit produces artifacts, not just reads them. Pairs the analyze loop in reverse: build a PoC → inspect it with any decompiler, or --emit asm/ir to see what the compiler generates. To emulate/run the output, pass it to emulate-code (shellcode/obj) or exec-observe (exe).
